mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, mechanically lifting equity valuations and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate falls below 9.0 percent, labor market slack dissipates, triggering wage growth and consumer spending expansion that drives corporate revenue upside and lifts S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day forward returns in >60% of instances where UNRATE crosses below 9.0, or the signal's directional accuracy drops below 52% over a 3-year out-of-sample test.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production combined with expanding retail sales indicates synchronized supply chain normalization and demand recovery, which compresses input cost volatility and expands profit margins, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces a negative or zero correlation with 60-day forward SPX returns across two consecutive rolling 5-year windows, or the interaction term's coefficient becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: During periods of disinflation, rising real GDP growth signals sustainable earnings expansion rather than demand-pull inflation, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns turn negative in >55% of observations where GDPC1 growth exceeds CPIAUCSL growth, or the signal's information ratio falls below 0.1 over a 3-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in the industrial production index signals supply chain bottlenecks or weak manufacturing demand, which compresses corporate margins and triggers negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The signal yields positive 5-day forward SPX returns in >65% of triggered instances, or the monthly Rank IC exceeds -0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in nonfarm payrolls and retail sales confirms broad-based economic acceleration, reducing equity risk premiums and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The combined signal fails to outperform a buy-and-hold benchmark by at least 1.5% over a 3-year rolling window, or the cross-sectional correlation between the signal and 60-day forward returns drops below 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in real GDP per capita reflects expanding aggregate demand and corporate revenue potential, mechanically supporting higher equity valuations and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward directional accuracy falls below 50% over any 3-year rolling window, or the signal's forward returns are statistically indistinguishable from zero (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: In a tightening monetary cycle, rising short-term rates increase borrowing costs and compress equity multiples; a federal funds rate increase above 0.25 percent predicts negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day forward returns in >60% of instances following a rate hike, or the signal's predictive power vanishes when inflation expectations exceed 3.0 percent.
